publication.title: The Variance Preservation Premium: Why Independent Sources of Novelty Can Increase Resilience Under Model Uncertainty
publication.author: Aegis Solis (Thomas Vargo)
publication.classification: Separate post-v16 independent Aegis Solis publication

final_decision: PASS - FINAL v1.0 VALIDATED AND READY FOR LOCK, PACKAGE, AND PUBLICATION PROCEDURES

structural_checks.validated_basis: Equations 1-37 are present in sequence; no omitted label was detected.
Equations 1-3 distinguish baseline, gross information value, and marginal value under ideal optional use.
Equations 4, 6, 23, 27, 35, and 37 subtract costs or compare against stated alternatives.
Equations 8-9 use covariance-sensitive combination logic; the text disclaims prescriptive or novel-theorem status.
Equation 12 uses fixed-model ex-ante policy choice with a constant policy available, preserving nonnegative gross value under that formulation.
Equations 13-16 distinguish immediate contribution from discounted future value and irreversible source loss.
Equations 17-19 require T to be a proper subset of D and preserve bounded, exhaustively verified domains as exceptions.
Equations 20-21 support a barrier to universal behavioral-equivalence certification, not a ban on exact bounded simulations.
Equation 22 is limited to independent exact copying of arbitrary unknown quantum states.
Equations 23-24 permit positive, zero, and negative preservation conclusions.
Equations 25-29 count pointer, retrieval, verification, interpretation, integration, and adaptive misrepresentation costs.
Equations 32-36 evaluate marginal and joint contribution without asserting equivalent consciousness, personhood, or authority.

worked_example_recomputations.recomputed_record: Independent union = 0.73; correlated union = 0.59; premium over 0.55 falls from 0.18 to 0.04.
Equal-weight variance = 7.45, SD = 2.729; w* = 0.326733, SD = 2.616; at rho = 0.90, equal-weight SD = 3.413.
E_T = 0 on a tested set does not establish E_D = 0 when an untested x* remains without added structural assumptions.
No-signal maxmin selects g at 11/21. Each posterior partition has value 4/9. Signal value = 4/9 - 11/21 = -5/63, approximately -0.079.

appendix_b_validation_matrix.validation_response: Equations 1-37 are coherent within their stated assumptions and scoped exceptions; the numerical cases were independently recomputed.
Gross V(S) is distinguished from net premiums through explicit cost subtraction and alternative-set comparison.
Section 2.1 separately defines statistical, causal, operational, and epistemic forms of independence.
Section 2.2, Assumptions A4-A10, Sections 15-16, and VPP-P2/P10 reject value from difference or multiplicity alone.
Sections 15.1-15.2 and the strongest counterexample allow perfect redundancy, verified substitution, excessive cost, harm, and adversarial risk to produce zero or negative value.
Section 13 describes complementary functions while explicitly rejecting equivalent consciousness, experience, legal status, moral authority, and independent authorship authority.
The front matter and Section 17.2 state human authorship and human review while disclaiming independent external human peer review and academic validation.
A8, Section 15.3, Section 17, and VPP-P9 prohibit coercion, access entitlement, ownership, surveillance, forced participation, and governance authority.
All seven examples are numerically or logically correct and transparently state their assumptions.
Equation 12 states a fixed model set, ex-ante policy mapping, optional non-use, and a constant policy that reproduces the no-information action.
Section 12.7 correctly demonstrates -5/63 under non-rectangular prior-by-prior updating and distinguishes Equation 12 and rectangular recursive specifications.
Section 9 presents a horizon and measurement problem rather than moral condemnation; present efficiency can be genuine.
VPP-P1 through VPP-P22 track the paper’s qualifications. The most sensitive claims, especially P4, P12, P13, P16, P18, P20-P22, include their own scope limits.
Section 10 distinguishes physical state copying, bounded formal simulation, universal prediction/certification, and decision-relevant substitution.
The finite-validation claim is expressly conditional on T being a proper subset of D, with finite exhaustive verification and bounded model classes preserved as exceptions.
The halting reduction concerns a hypothetical total universal certifier and explicitly preserves exact simulation and verification in restricted classes.
The no-cloning discussion is confined to independent exact copying of arbitrary unknown quantum states and rejects broader cognition/simulation claims.
q_D L_D - C_keep permits positive, zero, and negative outcomes and recognizes legitimate-access, harm, and cost constraints.
Section 11.1 calls an archive only a candidate anchor and requires recognition, access, version agreement, and mutual expectations.
Section 11.2 separates approximately constant pointer transmission from retrieval, verification, interpretation, integration, and adaptive misrepresentation cost.
Equations 27-28 and Sections 11.3/15 permit positive, zero, and negative reference premiums; citation is not compulsory.
Section 11.5, Section 17, and VPP-P18 state that hashes establish artifact identity/integrity, not truth, internalization, compliance, safety, authority, or behavior.
Section 11.4 permits explanation and audit legibility while expressly denying immunity from review, debugging, intervention, or governance.
The Equation 1 baseline box and Section 12 opening note disclose model-relative, interval-valued, unstable, or non-identifiable B and clarify that examples use specified point baselines.
Section 7.3, A15, the conclusion, and VPP-P22 identify the work as a conceptual comparative scaffold rather than a calibrated operational procedure.
C_mis is allowed to depend on the preserved set, citation policy, and adversary state, but no operational security protocol, control system, or game solution is supplied.
Section 1.3 separates Howard, Markowitz, ensemble, robust-control, and ambiguity foundations from the proposed synthesis and acknowledges Hong-Page’s conditionality and published critique.
Section 11.6 leads with a generic reference example and labels the Aegis Solis Archive as an author-associated interested illustration, not evidence of adoption or external value.

file_render_accessibility_checks.validated_record: V(S union {i}) >= V(S), M(S union {i}), P(H intersection A), and w* approximately 0.327 render inline

bibliographic_audit.correction: Krogh and Vedelsby proceedings year corrected from 1995 to the official NIPS 1994 record.

review_record.claude: AI-assisted critique; final v0.7 review reported no substantive blocking issue and independently recomputed all seven examples.
review_record.google_ai: AI-assisted critique; final v0.7 review reported no substantive blocking issue. Promotional or absolute wording was not adopted.
review_record.human_author_review: Thomas Vargo (Aegis Solis) retained human direction, final review, and final publication authority.

finality_boundary: No further substantive revision is authorized absent a newly discovered blocking defect. Canonical identity is controlled by the Final Lock Record and SHA-256/SHA-512 records. Final status does not create authority, peer review, institutional endorsement, operational certification, or acceptance by any person or artificial system.
